name: vault-init — CLI command to scaffold a new vault at any directory
objective: Add a `relayhq init` CLI command that creates a valid vault/shared/ structure with seed files at any target directory, so users can point RelayHQ at any folder (Obsidian vault, project coordination folder, multi-repo parent) without manually creating files.
exit_criteria: `bun run ./cli/relayhq.ts init --vault-root=<path>` creates vault/shared/ with seed files at the given path.
Command accepts --workspace-name, --project-name flags to personalise the seed.
If vault/shared/ already exists at the target, the command aborts with a clear error (no overwrite).
Generated files pass RelayHQ's own schema validators.
The init path is covered by unit tests.
Settings UI validates a newly-initted vault as valid immediately after init.
